response.justification: The candidate has peripheral relevance through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and early C++ work at National Instruments, but their career has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js web development. The core requirement — modern C++ or Rust for a production routing data pipeline — is not met: C++ experience is limited to a 6-month role from 2014-2015 and a university thesis project, Rust is absent entirely, and there is no experience with routing tiles, map data transformation, or navigation pipeline optimization at scale.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective, an open-source event-driven geo-data platform used in production by NGOs and conservation scientists
Distributed systems experience at scale from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, building low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across a broad range of environments including embedded and server-side systems
concerns: C++ experience is dated and thin — a 6-month NI role ending in 2015 and a bachelor thesis project; no evidence of modern C++ practices or commercial routing/geospatial C++ work
No Rust experience; primary production languages throughout the last decade are TypeScript and Node.js, which are not applicable to this role
GIS work is at an open-source/hobbyist level focused on point-of-interest data collection, not routing tile generation, map data transformation, or high-availability navigation pipeline engineering

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
